# PlanningPerm

> AI-powered planning permission guidance for UK homeowners. Check whether your home improvement project needs planning permission — free, in under 2 minutes.

## What PlanningPerm does

PlanningPerm helps UK homeowners understand their planning permission requirements before they build. It:

- Runs 20 automated site constraint checks (conservation area, Article 4 directions, listed building status, flood zone, Green Belt, AONB, TPO trees, highway proximity, and more)
- Calculates a real approval probability score based on the homeowner's council's published decision history (MHCLG PS2 data)
- Generates AI-drafted planning documents: Design & Access Statement, Planning Statement, Cover Letter, and Heritage Statement
- Covers all common householder project types: extensions, loft conversions, outbuildings, fences, solar panels, driveways, change of use, and more
- Is free to use and covers properties in England

## Who it's for

UK homeowners planning home improvements who want to know whether they need planning permission, what their chances of approval are, and what documents to prepare.

## Key facts about planning permission in England

- Permitted development (PD) rights allow many common home improvements without a full planning application
- Extensions under 4m (detached) or 3m (semi/terrace) to the rear generally don't need permission
- Conservation areas, Article 4 directions, and listed building status can remove or restrict PD rights
- Around 88% of householder planning applications in England are approved
- A planning application for a householder project costs £258 in England (as of 2025)
- A Lawful Development Certificate costs £103 and confirms a project is lawful under PD

## Start a check

To check a specific property: https://planningperm.com/dashboard/projects/new
